summary: Completed comprehensive frontend testing of Ad Preview & Visualization Dashboard iteration 2. All new campaign objective and ad format features working perfectly with excellent cross-platform compatibility.
passed_tests: Campaign Objective dropdown works perfectly with all 7 objectives (Traffic, Sales, Leads, Awareness, Engagement, App Installs, Video Views)
Awareness/Reach objective correctly hides CTA section and shows amber warning message
Engagement objective correctly hides CTA section as expected
Traffic, Sales, Leads, App Installs, Video Views objectives correctly show CTA section
All 9 platform tabs work: Facebook, Instagram, X/Twitter, LinkedIn, TikTok, YouTube, Pinterest, Snapchat, Google Ads
Platform-specific ad formats load correctly: Facebook (4 formats), TikTok (3 formats), YouTube (4 formats), Google Ads (3 formats)
Carousel format shows scrollable cards with dots navigation on Facebook and Instagram
Carousel card editor works: add/remove cards, edit headlines and descriptions per card
Story format renders correctly as vertical fullscreen (9:16 aspect ratio) for Facebook and Instagram
YouTube In-Stream format shows skip button correctly
YouTube Bumper format shows 6-second timer without skip button
YouTube Shorts format renders as vertical video correctly
Google Ads Search format shows search box, sponsored label, and proper layout
Google Ads Display format shows banner overlay layout with CTA button
Google Ads Shopping format displays 4-item product grid with ratings and prices
Snapchat Collection format shows 4-item grid at bottom as expected
TikTok TopView format shows large play button and TopView label
TikTok Spark Ad format displays correct 'Spark Ad' label
Preview indicator bar correctly shows current format name and objective badge
Format and objective selections persist correctly across platform switches
Mobile/Desktop device toggle still works seamlessly with new features
All form inputs work: Brand Name, Handle, Caption, Headline, Description update live in preview
Media uploader accepts image/* and video/* files as expected
All platform previews render correctly in both mobile and desktop modes

success_percentage.frontend: 100% (24/24 major features and components working flawlessly)
